title: 10 best herbal tinctures for acne
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for acne are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, often taken orally or applied topically to treat acne. They offer a natural alternative to traditional acne treatments and can help reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to acne. Examples of herbal tinctures used for acne include burdock root, echinacea, red clover, and dandelion. Burdock root, for instance, helps purify the blood and eliminate toxins, while echinacea boosts the immune system and reduces inflammation. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into their skincare routine, individuals can experience clearer, healthier skin, reducing the need for harsh chemical treatments and improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and the growth of bacteria that can cause acne. It also has astringent properties that can help to dry out excess oil on the skin, which can contribute to the development of acne. Additionally, yarrow tincture contains flavonoids and tannins that can help to reduce the production of sebum, which can clog pores and lead to acne. Furthermore, yarrow tincture has been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, which can help to soothe irritation and reduce the appearance of acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture helps reduce inflammation associated with acne, which is important for improving the skin's appearance and preventing scarring. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes, thus helping to prevent and treat breakouts. The antimicrobial activity comes from the essential oil components, including azulene and α-bisabolol.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture tightens and dries out the skin, reducing excess oil production and minimizing the appearance of pores. Tannins present in yarrow are responsible for this astringent property.

Wound healing: Yarrow tincture promotes tissue repair and reduces redness, which is beneficial for treating acne-related skin damage. The wound-healing properties are attributed to flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and alkaloids found in yarrow.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with acne, such as soreness or itching. This is due to the presence of lactones, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which have pain-relieving properties.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce acne-related redness and swelling.
Leaves: Contain antimicrobial compounds, assisting in preventing acne-causing bacteria growth.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants, aiding in reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Chop the yarrow into small pieces and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the yarrow, ensuring the plant material is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with acne because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and the severity of breakouts. The active compound in wormwood, called thujone, has been shown to inhibit the growth of P. acnes bacteria, which is one of the primary causes of acne. Additionally, wormwood tincture can help regulate sebum production, reducing the oily shine that can contribute to acne. Its calming and soothing effects can also help to alleviate the discomfort and itchiness associated with acne. Overall, wormwood tincture can be a valuable addition to a skincare routine for those looking for a natural and effective way to manage acne.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Wormwood tincture has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes, thus preventing the formation of new pimples (active constituents

Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, making it easier to manage the skin condition (active constituents
Wound-healing: Wormwood tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help improve the appearance of acne scars and prevent further skin damage (active constituents
Antioxidant: Wormwood tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can worsen acne (active constituents

Astringent: Wormwood tincture has astringent properties that help shrink pores, reduce excess oil production, and tighten the skin, making it less prone to acne and other skin conditions (active constituents
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the wormwood tincture plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne and soothing irritated skin.
Flowers: The flowers of the wormwood tincture plant contain potent compounds that help regulate sebum production, preventing acne breakouts and reducing the appearance of oily skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the wormwood tincture plant are a rich source of essential fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of acne scars.
Bark: The bark of the wormwood tincture plant has antimicrobial properties, which help to kill the bacteria that cause acne and prevent the spread of infection.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wormwood herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent herbs.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, label it with the ingredients and date, and keep in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry tincture helps with acne because it contains berberine, a chemical that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the bacteria that cause acne and decrease inflammation in the skin, which can help prevent and treat pimples and other acne symptoms. Additionally, barberry has been shown to have astringent qualities, which can help tighten pores and reduce the production of oil in the skin, further reducing the likelihood of acne breakouts. Overall, the natural properties of common barberry tincture make it a valuable tool in managing and improving acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Common barberry tincture has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria, preventing further breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are berberine and oxycanthine.

Anti-inflammatory: Barberry tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, promoting clearer and calmer skin. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is berberine.

Astringent: Barberry tincture has astringent properties that help shrink and tighten pores, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores and future acne breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins and berberine.

Antioxidant: Common barberry tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of acne scars. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are berberine, oxycanthine, and isoquinoline alkaloids.

Immune-boosting: Barberry tincture supports the immune system, which helps the body fight off infections and reduce inflammation, promoting overall skin health and reducing acne symptoms. The active constituent responsible for this property is primarily berberine.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing acne-causing bacteria and inflammation.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants and tannins, which can help reduce redness and soothe irritated skin, contributing to the management of acne.
Berries: Packed with essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals, these components can help regulate oil production, maintain skin health, and support acne treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried barberry bark and place it in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and use 1-2 dropperfuls daily for acne treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne. The herb contains high levels of flavonoids and carotenoids, which have been shown to help heal wounds, reduce scarring, and promote skin regeneration. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can help regulate sebum production, preventing the clogged pores that lead to acne. Its soothing and calming effects on the skin make it an effective natural remedy for treating and preventing acne breakouts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for acne as it can lessen the redness and swelling of pimples. This property is due to the active constituents, including flavonoids and triterpenes.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacteria responsible for acne. This is crucial for acne as it can help prevent the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that contributes to inflammatory acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include the antimicrobial compounds, such as thujone and camphor.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for acne as it can help speed up the recovery process and minimize scarring. This is due to the active constituents, including flavonoids and essential oils, that have been shown to promote tissue regeneration and collagen synthesis.

Astringent: Pot marigold tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the skin, which is important for acne as it can help reduce the size of pores and prevent the buildup of oil and bacteria. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for acne as it can help prevent the oxidative stress that can contribute to inflammation and acne formation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and carotenoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and soothe acne-prone skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are known to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can aid in preventing acne-causing bacteria from multiplying.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids and vitamin E, which can help maintain the skin's natural barrier function and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh pot marigold flowers and leaves, rinse thoroughly, and let dry.
Chop or crush the dried herbs to increase surface area, then place in a clean glass jar until 3/4 full.
Fill the jar with 80-proof vodka, ensuring all plant material is submerged, then secure the lid and shake gently.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it occasionally to promote extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the spent herbs, and store the final tincture in a sealed glass container away from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce redness and swelling of the skin. It contains high levels of catechins, which can help reduce sebum production and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria. White tea tincture also has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help eliminate acne-causing agents and promote healthy skin. Its soothing properties can help calm irritated skin and reduce the appearance of acne scars. Overall, white tea tincture can help maintain clear and healthy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for acne.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: White tea tincture can help reduce acne-causing bacteria, which is important because excessive growth of Propionibacterium acnes can lead to inflammation and skin breakouts. Active constituents like polyphenols, flavonoids, and tannins contribute to these antibacterial effects.

Anti-inflammatory properties: White tea tincture helps alleviate inflammation, which is crucial for treating acne as it reduces redness, swelling, and pain associated with inflamed pimples. The presence of catechins, such as EGCG, is responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antioxidant properties: White tea tincture contains high levels of antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for acne as it supports skin health and regeneration. The high concentration of polyphenols, particularly EGCG, contributes to its potent antioxidant properties.

Astringent properties: White tea tincture's astringent properties help tighten skin pores, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores and acne formation. The tannins in white tea are responsible for its astringent effects.

Regulating sebum production: White tea tincture can help regulate sebum production, which is important for acne as excessive sebum can mix with dead skin cells and clog pores. The presence of catechins, such as EGCG, may contribute to the regulation of sebum production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and fight acne-causing bacteria.
Buds: Rich in polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce acne.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and essential oils that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating acne.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and acne breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried herbal white tea leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the tea leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the recommended time, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the solid remains.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place or in the refrigerator for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tincture helps with acne because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, it has been shown to regulate the production of sebum, an oily substance that can clog pores and lead to breakouts. The cannabinoids in cannabis tincture, such as CBD, have been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat the bacteria that can cause acne. Furthermore, cannabis tincture may also help to reduce the appearance of acne scars, making it a potential holistic solution for those looking to improve the overall health and appearance of their skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for acne as it helps to decrease the redness and swelling of pimples. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly cannabinoids such as CBD and THC.

Antibacterial: Cannabis tincture has antibacterial properties that can help to kill the bacteria that cause acne. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is terpene called α-pinene.

Sebum regulation: Cannabis tincture can help regulate sebum production, which is important for acne as excess sebum can clog pores and lead to breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids such as CBD and THCV.

Anti-oxidant: Cannabis tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and terpenes.

Wound healing: Cannabis tincture can promote wound healing, which is important for acne as it can help to reduce the appearance of scars. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids such as CBD and CBG.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the cannabis tincture plant contain high levels of cannabinoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Leaves: The leaves of the cannabis tincture plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the appearance of acne.
Seeds: The seeds of the cannabis tincture plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help regulate oil production in the skin and reduce the likelihood of acne breakouts.
Buds: The buds of the cannabis tincture plant are rich in cannabinoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help treat acne and prevent further breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of cannabis flower and 1 ounce of calendula flowers, then combine them in a large mason jar.
Pour 2 cups of high-proof alcohol (such as vodka or grain alcohol) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged, then seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding the plant material and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle and store in a cool, dark place; apply a few drops to affected areas as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and the risk of infection in acne-prone skin. It also contains compounds that can improve skin texture and tone by enhancing collagen production, which can help to heal acne scars and prevent new ones from forming. Additionally, gotu kola has been shown to have a calming effect on the mind and can help to reduce stress, which can contribute to the development of acne. By addressing both the physical symptoms and underlying causes of acne, gotu kola tincture can provide a holistic approach to managing this common skin condition.
remedy_properties: Wound healing: Gotu kola tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for acne as it helps reduce the appearance of scars and blemishes caused by inflamed acne lesions. The active constituents responsible for this property are asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne. This is achieved through the inhibition of nitric oxide production and the modulation of prostaglandin synthesis by triterpene saponins like asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Antimicrobial: Gotu kola tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties against Propionibacterium acnes, the bacterium that contributes to acne development. This is attributed to the presence of tannins, saponins, and flavonoids, which disrupt bacterial growth and adhesion.

Collagen synthesis: Gotu kola tincture stimulates collagen synthesis, which is important for acne as it aids in the regeneration of damaged skin tissue and minimizes the appearance of acne scars. The active constituents responsible for this property are asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Venotonic: Gotu kola tincture has a venotonic effect, which improves blood circulation and reduces the accumulation of toxins and metabolic waste in the skin. This helps maintain healthy skin and reduces the likelihood of acne formation. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpene saponins, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal tea for acne due to their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling.
Stems: The stems of gotu kola contain antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of acne.
Rhizomes: Gotu kola rhizomes are rich in triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, making them useful for treating acne.
Flowers: The flowers of gotu kola contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce the appearance of acne.
Seeds: Gotu kola seeds are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help maintain the skin's natural barrier function and reduce the risk of acne.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried gotu kola leaves and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from light and heat, for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and bacteria that cause acne. The active ingredient in turmeric, curcumin, helps to decrease sebum production, which can clog pores and lead to breakouts. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties can help to reduce the appearance of acne scars and promote skin healing. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, turmeric tincture can help to clear up acne and improve overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to curcumin, helps reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin and other curcuminoids, combats the bacteria that cause acne.
Wound-healing: Turmeric tincture's wound-healing ability, facilitated by curcumin, aids in the faster recovery of acne-related skin lesions.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of turmeric tincture, owing to curcumin, helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of acne.
Sebum-regulating: Turmeric tincture's sebum-regulating property, due to curcumin, helps maintain optimal sebum production, preventing the clogging of pores that can lead to acne.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's rhizomes are used to make medicinal tea for acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce redness and fight acne-causing bacteria.

Leaves: Turmeric leaves are often used in medicinal teas for acne because they contain curcumin, a compound known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce acne inflammation.

Flowers: Turmeric flowers are a less common but still effective addition to medicinal teas for acne, as they contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce oil production, calm inflammation, and promote skin healing.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds are sometimes used in medicinal teas for acne due to their high oil content, which can help moisturize and soothe dry, irritated skin, while their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help reduce acne and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root and 1 cup of dried herbs (such as calendula, burdock root, and dandelion root) into small pieces.
Combine herbs and turmeric in a clean glass jar, then pour enough vodka or rum to cover the herbs by at least 2 inches.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herbs.

Label the bottle with the ingredients and date, then store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a few drops of the tincture to a cotton ball and gently dab it onto affected acne areas, avoiding the eye area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and the number of bacteria on the skin. This herbal remedy contains echinacea, which has been shown to improve skin conditions by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation. Additionally, it may help regulate sebum production, preventing clogged pores and reducing the likelihood of acne breakouts. Its soothing properties can also help calm irritated skin and promote faster healing of existing acne lesions, making it a valuable addition to any skincare routine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for acne as it can help decrease the redness and swelling of pimples. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.

Antibacterial: The tincture has antibacterial properties that can help kill the Propionibacterium acnes bacteria, which is one of the main causes of acne. This is due to the presence of active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids.

Wound-healing: Eastern purple coneflower tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for acne as it can help minimize scarring. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkamides and polyacetylenes.

Antioxidant: The tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can worsen acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Sebum regulation: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps regulate sebum production, which is important for acne as excessive sebum can lead to clogged pores and breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help reduce acne-related redness and swelling.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating acne-prone skin.
Buds: High in flavonoids, the buds aid in soothing irritated skin and promoting healing of acne lesions.
Stems: Packed with tannins, stems assist in reducing excess oil production and refining skin texture.
Seeds: A great source of essential fatty acids, seeds help maintain skin's natural barrier and support overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry basket.
Chop or grind 2 cups of fresh flowers, combining with 4 cups of 100-proof vodka in a glass jar, sealing tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a sterilized glass bottle, in a cool, dark location, for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL orally, up to three times a day, for acne treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with acne because it contains high levels of silica, a mineral that supports collagen production and promotes skin cell regeneration. This can help reduce the appearance of acne by promoting the growth of new, healthy skin cells and reducing inflammation. Additionally, horsetail is a natural astringent, which means it can help to tighten pores and reduce the production of excess oil, further reducing the likelihood of acne breakouts. The anti-bacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of horsetail tincture can also help to soothe and heal existing acne, making it an effective natural remedy for those looking to improve the health and appearance of their skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property assists in fighting acne-causing bacteria, mainly due to the existence of silica and other antimicrobial compounds.

Astringent: The astringent property of horsetail tincture helps to tighten pores and decrease oil production, reducing the likelihood of acne formation, which is attributed to the tannins present in horsetail.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of horsetail tincture aids in protecting skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier skin and reducing acne, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Collagen synthesis: Horsetail tincture promotes collagen synthesis, which contributes to skin repair and regeneration, minimizing the appearance of acne scars, owing to its high silica content.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of the horsetail tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for acne due to their high silica content, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the horsetail tincture plant are rich in antioxidants and can help to reduce the production of sebum, which can contribute to the development of acne.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the horsetail tincture plant contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and the number of acne-causing bacteria on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried horsetail herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the finished horsetail tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use as needed for acne.
